1→# Review: deepmind/1128.lean
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–25).
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1128`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1128`.
    18→- [ ] **Missing brief plain-English description of the problem in the module docstring.** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry [Er81b]. The plain-English problem description (the Erdős–Hajnal question about 2-colourings of products of ℵ₁ sets and monochromatic countable sub-products) appears only in the theorem docstring (lines 31–38), not in the module docstring itself. A brief summary should be added to the module docstring.
    19→- [x] Optional: bibliography entry present — [Er81b] Erdős 1981, well-formatted.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1128 ... end Erdos1128` (lines 29, 49).
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1128`.
    25→- [x] `open Cardinal` appears **before** the namespace block (line 27). Correct.
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    30→
    31→## 6. Theorem Statements
    32→
    33→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    34→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1128` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    35→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
    36→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 3 5]` (line 40).
    37→- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 31–38).
    38→- [x] Docstring references source paper: [Er81b,p.33].
    39→
    40→### `answer()` usage
    41→
    42→The problem asks a yes/no question: "Is it true that, in any 2-colouring of A × B × C, there must exist ... monochromatic ...?" — this is **Case 1**.
    43→
    44→- [x] The theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P`: correct Case 1 form.
    45→- [x] The problem has been disproved (by Prikry and Mills, 1978), so `answer(False)` is correct — "no" to the original yes/no question.
    46→- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: for any three types of cardinality ℵ₁ and any 2-colouring `f`, there exist countably infinite subsets whose product is monochromatic. With `answer(True)` this would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no". Correct polarity.
    47→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ (α β γ : Type) ...`, `∃ (A₁ : Set α) ...`) go **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters. Correct.
    48→
    49→## 7. Category Attribute
    50→
    51→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    52→- [x] Value is `research solved` — correct for a problem disproved by Prikry and Mills (1978).
    53→- [x] Not `research open`, so the `sorry`-free check does not apply.
    54→
    55→## 8. AMS Attribute
    56→
    57→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
    58→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `3` (Mathematical logic and foundations), `5` (Combinatorics).
    59→- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 3 < 5.
    60→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    61→- [x] Both `3` and `5` are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    62→
    63→## 9. Proof Bodies
    64→
    65→- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
    66→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (N/A — no definitions).
    67→- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
    68→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open problems (this is `research solved`; the docstring provides historical attribution, not a proof sketch).
    69→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    70→
    71→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    72→
    73→- N/A — no variants in this file.
    74→
    75→## 11. Code Quality
    76→
    77→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem use appropriate indentation).
    78→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `×`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`.
    79→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    80→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    81→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
    82→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    83→
    84→## 12. Build Verification
    85→
    86→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    87→- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.
    88→
    89→---
    90→
    91→## Mathematical Formalization Notes
    92→
    93→The formalization is faithful to the source problem:
    94→
    95→- **Sets of cardinality ℵ₁:** Modeled as arbitrary types `α β γ : Type` with `#α = aleph 1`, `#β = aleph 1`, `#γ = aleph 1`. Using types rather than sets within a fixed universe is standard Lean practice and is equivalent.
    96→- **2-colouring:** `f : α × β × γ → Fin 2` correctly represents a 2-colouring of the triple product.
    97→- **Countable sub-product:** The existential `∃ (A₁ : Set α) (B₁ : Set β) (C₁ : Set γ)` with `#A₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #B₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #C₁ = aleph 0` correctly requires each subset to be countably infinite.
    98→- **Monochromatic:** `∃ c : Fin 2, ∀ a ∈ A₁, ∀ b ∈ B₁, ∀ x ∈ C₁, f (a, b, x) = c` correctly asserts that the entire sub-product receives a single color.
    99→- **Answer polarity:** `answer(False)` is correct — the question "Is it true that ... must exist ...?" is answered "no" (disproved by Prikry and Mills, 1978).
   100→
   101→## Summary
   102→
   103→**Result: PASS (minor issue)**
   104→
   105→One minor non-conformance: the module docstring lacks an explicit plain-English problem description (§3). The problem statement lives entirely in the theorem docstring; a brief summary (e.g., "Erdős and Hajnal asked whether any 2-colouring of the product of three ℵ₁-sized sets must contain a monochromatic countably infinite sub-product") should be added to the module docstring. All other checklist items pass. The formalization is mathematically faithful to the source problem, with correct use of cardinal arithmetic and the `answer(False)` polarity reflecting the negative resolution.
   106→

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/1128.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–25).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1128`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1128`.
- [ ] **Missing brief plain-English description of the problem in the module docstring.** The module docstring contains only the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry [Er81b]. The plain-English problem description (the Erdős–Hajnal question about 2-colourings of products of ℵ₁ sets and monochromatic countable sub-products) appears only in the theorem docstring (lines 31–38), not in the module docstring itself. A brief summary should be added to the module docstring.
- [x] Optional: bibliography entry present — [Er81b] Erdős 1981, well-formatted.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1128 ... end Erdos1128` (lines 29, 49).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1128`.
- [x] `open Cardinal` appears **before** the namespace block (line 27). Correct.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A — no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1128` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 3 5]` (line 40).
- [x] Theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 31–38).
- [x] Docstring references source paper: [Er81b,p.33].

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks a yes/no question: "Is it true that, in any 2-colouring of A × B × C, there must exist ... monochromatic ...?" — this is **Case 1**.

- [x] The theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P`: correct Case 1 form.
- [x] The problem has been disproved (by Prikry and Mills, 1978), so `answer(False)` is correct — "no" to the original yes/no question.
- [x] The RHS (`P`) formalizes the question as originally asked: for any three types of cardinality ℵ₁ and any 2-colouring `f`, there exist countably infinite subsets whose product is monochromatic. With `answer(True)` this would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` means "no". Correct polarity.
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ (α β γ : Type) ...`, `∃ (A₁ : Set α) ...`) go **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters. Correct.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — correct for a problem disproved by Prikry and Mills (1978).
- [x] Not `research open`, so the `sorry`-free check does not apply.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `3` (Mathematical logic and foundations), `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order: 3 < 5.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `3` and `5` are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (N/A — no definitions).
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open problems (this is `research solved`; the docstring provides historical attribution, not a proof sketch).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants in this file.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem use appropriate indentation).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `×`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all well under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

## Mathematical Formalization Notes

The formalization is faithful to the source problem:

- **Sets of cardinality ℵ₁:** Modeled as arbitrary types `α β γ : Type` with `#α = aleph 1`, `#β = aleph 1`, `#γ = aleph 1`. Using types rather than sets within a fixed universe is standard Lean practice and is equivalent.
- **2-colouring:** `f : α × β × γ → Fin 2` correctly represents a 2-colouring of the triple product.
- **Countable sub-product:** The existential `∃ (A₁ : Set α) (B₁ : Set β) (C₁ : Set γ)` with `#A₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #B₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #C₁ = aleph 0` correctly requires each subset to be countably infinite.
- **Monochromatic:** `∃ c : Fin 2, ∀ a ∈ A₁, ∀ b ∈ B₁, ∀ x ∈ C₁, f (a, b, x) = c` correctly asserts that the entire sub-product receives a single color.
- **Answer polarity:** `answer(False)` is correct — the question "Is it true that ... must exist ...?" is answered "no" (disproved by Prikry and Mills, 1978).

## Summary

**Result: PASS (minor issue)**

One minor non-conformance: the module docstring lacks an explicit plain-English problem description (§3). The problem statement lives entirely in the theorem docstring; a brief summary (e.g., "Erdős and Hajnal asked whether any 2-colouring of the product of three ℵ₁-sized sets must contain a monochromatic countably infinite sub-product") should be added to the module docstring. All other checklist items pass. The formalization is mathematically faithful to the source problem, with correct use of cardinal arithmetic and the `answer(False)` polarity reflecting the negative resolution.

    31→/--

    33→$\aleph_1$. Is it true that, in any $2$-colouring of $A \times B \times C$, there must exist
    34→$A_1 \subset A$, $B_1 \subset B$, $C_1 \subset C$, all of cardinality $\aleph_0$, such that
    35→$A_1 \times B_1 \times C_1$ is monochromatic?
    36→

    38→(unpublished), as reported by Todorčević and Komjáth.

    44→      ∃ (A₁ : Set α) (B₁ : Set β) (C₁ : Set γ),
    45→        #A₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #B₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #C₁ = aleph 0 ∧
    46→        ∃ c : Fin 2, ∀ a ∈ A₁, ∀ b ∈ B₁, ∀ x ∈ C₁, f (a, b, x) = c := by

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 1128

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1128](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1128)

[Er81b] Erdős, P., *Problems and results on finite and infinite combinatorial analysis*, p.33.
-/

open Cardinal

namespace Erdos1128

/--
A problem of Erdős and Hajnal [Er81b,p.33]. Let $A$, $B$, $C$ be three sets of cardinality
$\aleph_1$. Is it true that, in any $2$-colouring of $A \times B \times C$, there must exist
$A_1 \subset A$, $B_1 \subset B$, $C_1 \subset C$, all of cardinality $\aleph_0$, such that
$A_1 \times B_1 \times C_1$ is monochromatic?

The answer is no. This was disproved by Prikry and Mills in 1978
(unpublished), as reported by Todorčević and Komjáth.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 3 5]
theorem erdos_1128 : answer(False) ↔
    ∀ (α β γ : Type) (_ : #α = aleph 1) (_ : #β = aleph 1) (_ : #γ = aleph 1)
      (f : α × β × γ → Fin 2),
      ∃ (A₁ : Set α) (B₁ : Set β) (C₁ : Set γ),
        #A₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #B₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #C₁ = aleph 0 ∧
        ∃ c : Fin 2, ∀ a ∈ A₁, ∀ b ∈ B₁, ∀ x ∈ C₁, f (a, b, x) = c := by
  sorry

end Erdos1128

content.text: The fix is straightforward — add a brief plain-English description to the module docstring.

input.new_string:
/-!
# Erdős Problem 1128

Erdős and Hajnal asked whether any 2-colouring of the product of three sets of cardinality ℵ₁
must contain a monochromatic countably infinite sub-product. This was disproved by Prikry and
Mills (1978).

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1128](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1128)

[Er81b] Erdős, P., *Problems and results on finite and infinite combinatorial analysis*, p.33.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 1128

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1128](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1128)

[Er81b] Erdős, P., *Problems and results on finite and infinite combinatorial analysis*, p.33.
-/

open Cardinal

namespace Erdos1128

/--
A problem of Erdős and Hajnal [Er81b,p.33]. Let $A$, $B$, $C$ be three sets of cardinality
$\aleph_1$. Is it true that, in any $2$-colouring of $A \times B \times C$, there must exist
$A_1 \subset A$, $B_1 \subset B$, $C_1 \subset C$, all of cardinality $\aleph_0$, such that
$A_1 \times B_1 \times C_1$ is monochromatic?

The answer is no. This was disproved by Prikry and Mills in 1978
(unpublished), as reported by Todorčević and Komjáth.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 3 5]
theorem erdos_1128 : answer(False) ↔
    ∀ (α β γ : Type) (_ : #α = aleph 1) (_ : #β = aleph 1) (_ : #γ = aleph 1)
      (f : α × β × γ → Fin 2),
      ∃ (A₁ : Set α) (B₁ : Set β) (C₁ : Set γ),
        #A₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #B₁ = aleph 0 ∧ #C₁ = aleph 0 ∧
        ∃ c : Fin 2, ∀ a ∈ A₁, ∀ b ∈ B₁, ∀ x ∈ C₁, f (a, b, x) = c := by
  sorry

end Erdos1128

lines: +Erdős and Hajnal asked whether any 2-colouring of the product of three sets of cardinality ℵ₁
+must contain a monochromatic countably infinite sub-product. This was disproved by Prikry and
[Er81b] Erdős, P., *Problems and results on finite and infinite combinatorial analysis*, p.33.

content.text: Added a brief plain-English problem description to the module docstring, which was the only issue flagged in the review. The description summarizes the Erdős–Hajnal question and its negative resolution by Prikry and Mills.
